steps.title: Spot the relationship between the claims
steps.content: The sentence makes a claim about striking differences in behavior, then shifts to explain what those differences actually are. The second part elaborates the first—it illustrates the differences with specific details about each object.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule for elaborations
steps.content:
A colon introduces an elaboration or explanation of the preceding statement.

Option D uses the colon to frame the explanation of the differences, with “while” signaling the contrast between the two objects' opposite properties (one active, one dormant).

Option A breaks the thought into separate sentences; Option B uses a comma + “while” to create subordination instead of elaboration; Option C joins clauses with a semicolon without the explanatory force.

The colon directly introduces the contrasting details that explain the claimed differences.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
